title: luzula parviflora subsp. yezoensis
intro: Luzula parviflora subsp. yezoensis, also known as Yezo woodrush, is a rare subspecies of the Luzula parviflora plant, native to the northern island of Hokkaido in Japan. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Yezo woodrush include reducing fever and pain, as well as treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Luzula parviflora subsp. yezoensis are typically made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
